title: bromus rigidus subsp. maximus
intro: Bromus rigidus subsp. maximus, also known as tall cheatgrass or big bent, is a subspecies of the perennial grass species Bromus rigidus. It is native to the southwestern United States and is often found in dry, open areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of Bromus rigidus subsp. maximus can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, and can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams.
